channel.title: Projects on Shubham
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on Shubham

item.title: Generating realistic Human Faces using GANs
item.description: In this project, we implemented generative adversarial network to generate realistic looking human faces. A GAN is simply described as a game between two neural networks to beat each other. Dataset Used: Flickr-Faces-HQ Dataset (FFHQ) dataset This dataset contains 52k high-quality PNG images relating to human faces at 512x512 resolution. We implemented Discriminator with a four-layerd architecture strided with batch normalisation(except input layer) and LeakyRelu activations with sigmoid on the final layer.

item.title: 911 Calls Analysis
item.description: In this project, I analyzed and visualized 911 calls data from Montgomery County, Pennsylvania. Dataset Used: Kaggel 911 Calls Dataset (https://www.kaggle.com/mchirico/montcoalert) I visualized and analyzed this data using different well-known libraries in python, such as pandas, numpy, seaborn, plotly, matplotlib. I used folium for visualizsing the coordinates latitude and longitude on a map. I tried to look into various factors and reasons for all the 911 calls and tried to find correlations between different factors.

item.title: Covid-19 Future Forecasting
item.description: In this project, which was later adapted as part of a research paper, I followed these steps: Data Collection and Preprocessing: I collected data from the following sources: “Ministry of Health and Family Welfare India” website (https://www.mohfw.gov.in/) “World Health Organization (WHO) website (https://covid19.who.int/table) “Our World In Data” website (https://ourworldindata.org/coronavirus) “Kaggle” website (https://www.kaggle.com/) I performed data cleaning and preprocessing as a lot of data was missing in all the datasets.

item.title: Java For Everyone
item.description: I worked on this project during my undergraduate education. It was a fun project that I started on my own when I was learning Java programming language. The goal with this project was to provide everyone who wants to learn about Java Programming language, with all the necessary resources just a single click away. This app was divided into sub-sections which were as follows: Tutorial Section Practice Question Section Sample Codes Section Motivation Section The tutorial section of this android application contains basic java tutorials for beginners.

item.title: React Mini Apps
item.description: In this project I worked on several mini react.js projects on my learning journey of react.js. These applications are smaller components which can be used in large-scale websites. These applications include: Birthday reminder app Controlled forms Github users app Learning react.js has been very interesting and fun. Currently I am learning about react.js and the MERN stack for Full Stack Web Development.
